summary.intent: Refactor the gossamer Electron desktop app into a VS Code extension to reduce install friction and improve context-switching between code editing and Claude session viewing. Then iteratively improve the extension with session detail navigation, VS Code theming, compact UI, checkpoint diffs, and a native tree view.
summary.outcome: Successfully refactored gossamer into a monorepo with packages/core (extracted indexing logic) and packages/vscode (VS Code extension). The extension auto-detects repos via .entire/settings.json, spawns the gossamer server as a child process, shows sessions in a webview, opens session details in new tabs, and displays a native VS Code tree view of checkpoints with a working diff viewer.

repo: gossamer architecture: Entire.io CLI writes session data to a git branch → gossamer reads that branch and indexes into SQLite via Prisma + better-sqlite3
Detection of Entire.io-enabled repos is done by checking for .entire/settings.json at the repo root
The CheckpointIdGitOidJoin table maps checkpointId (12-char hex) to git OIDs; the git OID's repo is found via the session's cwd field, not a global repoDir
The server's /diff and /diff-stats endpoints accept a localPath query param to select the correct repo — the new /file endpoint needed the same pattern
better-sqlite3 is a native module compiled against the system Node.js (MODULE_VERSION 141), which conflicts with VS Code's bundled Electron Node.js (MODULE_VERSION 140)

code.finding: Originally only listed extension.ts and GossamerPanel.ts in include array — SessionDetailPanel.ts and diffUtils.ts were never compiled, causing silent runtime import failures. Fixed to src/**/*.ts with src/webview excluded.

process.execPath points to VS Code's Electron binary, not system node. Must use execFileSync('which', ['node']) to get system Node.js for spawning the gossamer server to avoid native module version mismatches.

Relative path for root ('src/webview') resolves relative to CWD when run via npm workspace, not the package directory. Must use resolve(__dirname, 'src/webview') with __dirname from fileURLToPath(import.meta.url).

Scroll-to-bottom effect must check position inline at effect-fire time (scrollHeight - scrollTop - clientHeight < 80) rather than tracking with a separate scroll listener, because Mantine ScrollArea viewport ref is null when the [] dep effect runs.

New /api/v2/checkpoints/:id/file endpoint must look up the repo path from the session's cwd (via CheckpointIdGitOidJoin → ShadowSession join) rather than using the global repoDir variable, because checkpoints can belong to any repo.

workflow: VS Code webview scripts must have type='module' when Vite is used, since Vite outputs ESM — missing this attribute causes the browser to silently skip the script
VS Code webview reloads from scratch when dragged to a new window; server_ready must be re-sent via onDidChangeViewState, not just on initial startup

VS Code injects CSS custom properties (--vscode-editor-background, etc.) and body classes (vscode-dark/vscode-light) into webviews automatically — use these for theme integration with forceColorScheme on MantineProvider

Native diff editor is opened via vscode.commands.executeCommand('vscode.diff', leftUri, rightUri, title) with real file URIs for syntax highlighting; content provider approach is fragile due to URI encoding issues
Temp files for diff views should be chmod 444 so VS Code opens them as read-only

friction: tsconfig.extension.json explicit include list silently excluded new files — caused a blank diff view with no error message, required tracing all the way through compiled output to find
Content provider approach for diff views was attempted first and abandoned due to blank pages; root cause (tsconfig) wasn't found until after switching to temp files
Server readiness on first open caused 'error loading sessions' — the schema push takes several seconds and the webview fetched immediately
npm workspace -w flag requires the exact package name (gossamer-vscode) not the directory name (vscode)
Debug output from the extension host goes to Debug Console, not the Output panel — easy to miss
The /file endpoint silently returned empty content when the gitOid existed but the repo path was wrong (used global repoDir instead of session cwd)
open_items: packages/core CLI tool deferred — currently only exposes module API, user mentioned possibly packaging as its own CLI later
Electron app is intentionally broken/deprecated but not removed — can clean up after VS Code extension is stable
better-sqlite3 native module rebuild story not fully resolved for distribution — needs prebuilts or rebuild tooling for publishing to VS Code marketplace
VS Code marketplace review process not yet addressed — needed before public distribution
Optional background daemon support deferred (deferred to v2 if users request always-on capture when VS Code is closed)
Webview UI reevaluation deferred — kept existing Mantine components as-is, user said 'we can reevaluate later'
